title: gitbox configuration v2
description: Configuration file for gitbox — manages Git multi-account environments across multiple providers (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, Forgejo, Bitbucket).

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI for editor validation and autocompletion.

global.description: Global settings: root directory for clones, SSH and GCM credential configuration.

folder.description: Root directory for all git repositories. Supports ~ prefix for home directory or absolute paths.

periodic_sync.description: Automatic periodic fetch interval. When set, the GUI will run 'git fetch' on all repos at this interval.

window.description: GUI window position and size. Saved automatically when the GUI is closed, restored on next launch.

x.description: Window horizontal position (pixels from left edge of screen).

y.description: Window vertical position (pixels from top edge of screen).

compact_window.description: GUI compact-view window position and size. Saved automatically when switching to compact mode.

x.description: Window horizontal position (pixels from left edge of screen).

y.description: Window vertical position (pixels from top edge of screen).

view_mode.description: Last active GUI view mode. Restored on next launch.

credential_ssh.description: Global SSH platform defaults. Presence indicates SSH is available on this platform.

ssh_folder.description: Path to the SSH configuration directory. Supports ~ prefix. Defaults to ~/.ssh.

credential_gcm.description: Git Credential Manager (HTTPS) platform defaults. Presence indicates GCM is available.

helper.description: Credential helper type. Typically 'manager' for GCM.

credential_store.description: OS-specific credential storage backend. wincredman = Windows Credential Manager, keychain = macOS Keychain, secretservice = Linux GNOME Keyring / KWallet.

credential_token.description: Token/PAT authentication platform defaults. Presence indicates token auth is available. Tokens are stored in ~/.config/gitbox/credentials/.

accounts.description: Map of account identifiers to account configurations. Each account represents WHO you are on a git server — one unique (hostname, username) pair. The map key is a human-friendly ID (e.g., 'github-personal') also used as the default first-level clone folder.

additionalProperties.description: A Git provider account — identity and credentials for one server login.

provider.description: Git hosting provider type. Determines which API is used for discovery, migration, and mirrors. Use 'generic' for providers without API integration.

url.description: Provider base URL (scheme + host only, no path). E.g., 'https://github.com' or 'https://git.example.org'.

username.description: Account username on the Git provider. Used for authentication and API calls.

name.description: Default git user.name for commits in repos under this account.

email.description: Default git user.email for commits in repos under this account.

default_credential_type.description: Default authentication method for repos in this account. Repos inherit this unless they override it. 'gcm' = Git Credential Manager (HTTPS), 'ssh' = SSH key pair, 'token' = Personal Access Token.

ssh.description: SSH authentication configuration for this account.

host.description: SSH config Host alias (e.g., 'gh-MyGitHubUser'). Used in clone URLs: git@gh-MyGitHubUser:org/repo.git

hostname.description: Real SSH server hostname (e.g., 'github.com'). Written to ~/.ssh/config.

key_type.description: SSH key type to generate. ed25519 is recommended.

gcm.description: Git Credential Manager configuration for this account.

provider.description: GCM provider hint for the OAuth authentication flow.

useHttpPath.description: If true, GCM scopes credentials by full HTTP path (one credential per repo). If false, credentials are shared per domain/user. Maps to git config credential.useHttpPath.

sources.description: Map of source identifiers to source configurations. Each source defines WHAT you clone from an account. The map key is used as the default first-level clone folder (unless overridden by the 'folder' field).

additionalProperties.description: A clone source — references an account and lists repositories to manage.

account.description: References an account key from the 'accounts' section. Defines which credentials and identity to use.

folder.description: Override the first-level clone folder. If omitted, the source map key is used. Path under global.folder.

repos.description: Map of repository keys to repo configurations. Keys use 'org/repo' format (e.g., 'myorg/my-project', 'MyUser/my-repo'). The org part becomes the second-level folder, the repo part becomes the third-level (clone) folder.

additionalProperties.description: Configuration for a single repository. All fields are optional — repos inherit defaults from the account.

credential_type.description: Override authentication method for this repo. If omitted, inherits from account's default_credential_type.

name.description: Override git user.name for this specific repo.

email.description: Override git user.email for this specific repo.

id_folder.description: Override the second-level directory (org folder). Default: the part before '/' in the repo key. Always relative.

clone_folder.description: Override the third-level directory (clone folder). Default: the part after '/' in the repo key. If absolute (starts with '/', '~', or '../'), replaces the entire clone path, ignoring source folder and id_folder.

mirrors.description: Map of mirror group identifiers to mirror configurations. Each mirror group defines a pair of accounts between which repos are mirrored (push or pull). Mirror repos are NOT cloned locally — they are remote-only backups managed via provider APIs.

additionalProperties.description: A mirror group — pairs two accounts and lists repos mirrored between them.

account_src.description: References an account key from the 'accounts' section. Source account in the mirror pair.

account_dst.description: References an account key from the 'accounts' section. Destination account in the mirror pair. Must differ from account_src.

repos.description: Map of repository keys to mirror repo configurations. Keys use 'org/repo' format matching the repo name on the origin account.

additionalProperties.description: Configuration for a single mirrored repository.

direction.description: Mirror direction. 'push' = origin server pushes to backup. 'pull' = backup server pulls from origin.

origin.description: Which account holds the source of truth. 'src' = account_src, 'dst' = account_dst.

target_repo.description: Full name of the repo on the backup account (e.g., 'org/repo'). Defaults to the same as the map key.

last_sync.description: RFC3339 timestamp of last known successful sync.

error.description: Last error message from setup or sync.
